what that means to them and what they need to do for their hormone type. Secondly, don't be afraid to get personal. So when I reverse engineered all of our questions, there were three different kinds. The first one is a black and white question. Pretty straightforward, how old are you? What's your gender? How many kids do you have? Whatever that is. Then you have agitation questions. So these are the questions that ask about their problem. So this one is like what's your body shape? Do you have belly fat since turning 55? Are you more apple shaped? Do you have pronounced curves in the hips, butt, and thighs areas? Or do you have normal weight and shape for a woman? That's usually not the case. So what you can see here is that when people answer these kinds of questions, they're reaffirming and recognizing the depth of their problem. And then we have what's called primer questions. These are the questions that future pace them and give them hope that they can really solve their problem.
